entries.zone_name: Drayek's Chamber: Throne of the Kromise King
entries.polished_md:
## Positioning

It is recommended that you pull Darathar into the water, and have casters positioned at maximum range to avoid incurable trauma damage.

## Self-Heals & Stuns

He will cast a raid-wide **stun** and heals for **25% of his maximum health** three times during the fight, at **75%**, **50%** and **25%** health. This means:

- At **75%** health, he will heal to full health.
- At **50%** he heals to **75%** health.
- At **25%** he heals back to **50%** health.

After that point, he will no longer heal himself.

## Flame of Ages

His special attack, [Flame of Ages](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_of_Ages), is an **elemental area-of-effect** spell which does a high amount of direct damage and has a damage-over-time component. He casts this approximately every **50 seconds**.

> Each member should have **elemental cure potions** to ensure they can cure themselves in case a healer is unable to cure them immediately.

## Per-Instance Heal Behaviour

The self-heals only occur **once per instance** at each health threshold, not per encounter. For example, if you get Darathar below **50%** and then the raid wipes, Darathar will only heal once during subsequent fights. If the raid wipes after taking Darathar to **25%** health, he will not heal and stun again in that instance.

## Pull & Positioning

- Sneak the main raid force past Mayong and hug one of the corners behind his coffin.
- Keep the **main tank** at max distance from the raid.

## Mechanics

Mayong has two PBAOE spells that he casts every so often that you'll want to avoid.

> One of them, **Blanket of Eternal Night**, heals Mayong the closer you are to him.

- **Melee DPS** should be jousting before either of these two hit — keep an eye on your timers.

### Adds

- Every so often Mayong will spawn two **'a vampiric protector'** adds.
- These should be picked up and burned down from the raid force, then refocus attention on Mayong.

### Unholy Strike

- Casts an ability called **'Unholy Strike'** that heals him for **11,500,000** damage.

- Loot her corpse for the [Essence Shard of Soul](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_Shard_of_Soul) needed for [Fire and Ice](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fire_and_Ice).
- Clear the beach of all other mobs before engaging Mjolni, as all remaining will immediately aggro when Mjolni is pulled.
Speak to Gragak to gain entrance to [The Deserted Mine](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Deserted_Mine).

## Clearing

Clear all drakes on the lower platform in a (clockwise or counterclockwise) direction, leaving the **droags** for a second clearing, as these have a shorter respawn time.

Move raid to top of stairs and **body pull** the drakes, again in a clockwise or counterclockwise manner. All can be pulled without aggro'ing Harla Dar.

## Pull

Once all drakes and droags are clear, pull **Harla Dar** to the blocked wall behind the area where she stands (the zone out) and be careful of **knockback**.

## Entry

To get to him, use the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) that is dropped by the previous encounter to **break the barrier at entrance to the room**.

## Opening AoE

The first AOE is a beast that has to be **jousted**. Timing it is a bit hard because it varies slightly.

> Bring a lot of **trauma cure potions** because the healers won't have time to cure trauma from everyone that gets hit (and with people jousting, they might be out of range too).

## Pull

> **Pet pull does not work** because the mob cannot be attacked until someone enters the room (he will auto-aggro as soon as you enter).

Use a **dedicated puller** to pull him out of room to the tank spot. Try to keep as many people outside the AOE range on pull as possible and **debuff him fast**. Be sure to use all debuffs that debuff the **melee damage** of him; he can hit really hard and nearly all damage is melee.

## Phase 2 — Pillars at 35%

When he gets to **35%**, some **pillars** will pop in the room where his original position was. You will get zone-wide messages for that.

> While these are up he will **reflect all damage** — so just debuff and heal at that time.

The player(s) that have the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) (should be some **DPS classes**, no healer or other class that is needed to keep tank up during that time) should move into the room.

- It helps to have **multiples of these staffs** (they are not no-zone) because it increases your chances of destroying the pillar fast
- Keep collecting them for your next tries if you cannot kill him first time

Start using the staffs on the pillars until the pillars are destroyed, which can take some time. You will get a zone-wide message for that again.

## Finish

When the pillars are destroyed, finish off named and you are done with the zone.

- Mob will cast **Touch of the Faceless** on **MT** every **40s**, which will do between **40K and 80K damage**. If needed, have **OT's Intercede** to absorb Touch of the Faceless.
- Also has a **Mana Burn** that will instantly drain power of your party.
His treasure chest will drop right in the middle of him. Just right-click his corpse and select **'loot'** to remove his corpse from in-game and make his loot accessible.

If you have finished the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), he will offer to teleport you to the entrance of [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).

This is possibly the hardest hitting named in Veeshan's Peak. It is recommended that you have at least **3 good heals** in the Main Tank group. **Healers** should try to get **Shield of Rainbow Hues** to block Druushk's periodic **Fear AE**.

## Positioning

- Everybody except the **tank** stays on the little island that is circling round Hoshkar's "main" isle. You are protected there by an **aura**.
- The **tank** stays on the main isle kiting Hoshkar in range of the little moving island.

## Notes

- If Hoshkar goes in the **lava** he **stoneskins**.
- **Pets** are fine in this fight.

## Pull

Kluzen is the **easiest** of the named monsters in [Veeshan's Peak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Veeshan's_Peak). Before the fight, make sure to clear all of the nearby trash mobs, as they are roamers — don't forget the [Drakota](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Drakota) flying overhead.

## Mechanics

Kluzen is practically a **straight burn**. He does a large AOE called **wingbeat** (**45 seconds**) that must be **announced and jousted**, but that is all.

## Zone-In

- When you enter you are in a zone in/zone out room which leads to gigantic stairs. You must use the side area of the stairs to walk up into the main lair.
- When you first enter the zone, trakanon will greet you from far above the platform, then fly down. At this point you can engage trakanon.

## Requirements

- You must have a **chelsith stone** with you to have any chance to succeed. It will drop in the leviathan encounter in-game.

## The Orb

- Once the fight begins, you will notice the holder of a chelsith stone can touch the **orb altar** on the far side of the platform to energize it.
- Once energized, if you stay within **14m** of the energized orb structure, you are protected from many of trakanon's more powerful abilities.

> The key to winning this encounter is to realize you must all fight within **14m** of the activated orb on the far side of the platform. Trakanon's arcane maelstrom breath weapon is far too lethal outside this protective area. In addition trakanon's knockback abilities will prove problematic, and all but one of them is negated when you are close to this orb object (if it's energized).

## Mechanics

### Wing Buffet & Tail Swipe

- Trakanon is a **level 87 warlock**. He will periodically cast **Wing Buffet** and **Tail Swipe**.
- **Wing Buffet** will **STUN** everyone in front of him for **2.5 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK** everyone back UNLESS they are within **14m** of the energized orb.
- **Tail Swipe** does some damage, and stuns players for about **3 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK BACK** any players that are NOT within **14m** of the energized orb.
- Trakanon is **immune to all control spells**, and will **double attack on melee 10% more** than normal.

### Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord

- Trakanon has **Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord** on him.
- On take damage **10% of the time**, players will be hit with **Pestilential Pause**. This does a slight amount of damage to the player and for the next **10 seconds**, all the players spells take much longer to cast, and are basically at **half effectiveness** (heals and damage are half as effective as normal). Also your attack speed is reduced considerably during this time. This effect is **not dispellable**.
- Also, **2% of the time** trakanon is hit with a hostile spell, he will heal himself for **2%**.

### Prophetic Scrutiny

- Trakanon will keep **PROPHETIC SCRUTINY** on **one player** at all times.
- With this effect on you, any spells you cast are interrupted, if the spell is interruptable.
- Every time you start to cast a spell you will be hit with **PROPHETIC BOLT**, which does a small amount of damage and interrupts the spell you are starting to cast.
- This should be active on one player in the raid at all times.

### Prescient Gaze

- Trakanon will keep **Prescient gaze** on **all players** at all times. It should never be off you for more than a few seconds even if you die and are rezzed back in.
- It causes all players to **mana burn**, using health whenever they use mana.
- It also causes players to have **prescient intervention** cast on them **8% of the time** they try and cast a beneficial spell. Prescient intervention interrupts the spell they are trying to cast, and stifles the player for **2 seconds**, and does a small amount of damage to the players mana pool.

### Mark of Repulsion

- One player will generally have **MARK OF REPULSION** on him/her. This spell will go off at some random time, never less than **30 secs** after it was placed on you. It has an **hourglass icon**.
- When it goes off that player will be hit with **static repulsion**, a targeted AOE that WILL knock back all players within **22m** of the person who had Mark of Repulsion on him/her.
- You are **NEVER protected from this knockback**, even if near the orb.

> You must **move away from your raid party** so as not to endanger them with your Mark of Repulsion/Static Repulsion combo.

### Arcane Maelstrom

- **3.5 seconds AFTER** static repulsion goes off, Trakanon will cast **Arcane Maelstrom**.
- A powerful aoe that does **heat damage**, does damage to a random item in everyone's inventory, and does **massive MAGIC BASED health and mana damage** UNLESS you are close to the energized orb.
- You avoid MAGIC BASED MANA AND HEALTH DAMAGE **only if you are near the energized orb**.

### Fetid Cloud & Toxic Tempest

- Trakanon will periodically cast **Fetid Cloud** and **Toxic Tempest** as well.
- **Fetid Cloud** does **disease based** health damage — the closer you are to trak the more disease damage you take, and slows your attack speed.

> If you have **Toxic Tempest** still on you when **fetid cloud** hits you, you will take ADDITIONAL MASSIVE **poison based** health damage.

- **Toxic Tempest** works similarly to Fetid Cloud — does range relative, **poison based** health damage to all players.

> If you have **fetid cloud** on you when **Toxic tempest** hits you, you will take additional MASSIVE **disease damage** as well. Toxic tempest slows your movement rate as well.

### Enrage Timer (Lava)

- **800 seconds** after the raid fight begins, the platform you fight on begins to lower into the lava.
- This takes about **20-30 secs**, after that time you will be forced to fight trakanon in the lava, and it won't likely work well for you.
- If you hop away from the platform, you won't be protected by the orb so that's not really an option.
- If you stay near the orb, you will be covered in lava so that's not really an option.
- You have only about **820 secs** to win this fight.

## Reset

- If trakanon dies, the platform will rise.
- If trakanon resets, the platform will rise.

## Overview

This is really a **burn fight**.

## Sacs (every ~30s)

Tairiza spawn **"sacs"** approximately every **30 seconds**. These sac will land on a random person in the raid which will incapacitate them as well as kill them over time.

- Have **ranged DPS** and **healers** to the left or right of the door with a **Ranged DPS main assist** calling the sacs and making the ranged dps cycle between killing the sacs then immediately back on the named.
- **Melee DPS** needs to stand right under the big butt of the Widow Mistress and target the mob directly and **BURN IT DOWN**.
- If a sac spawns on **melee dps** or the **Main Tank** it's easy for the Melee dps to just TAB, burn the sac down, then resume beating the spider.

## Add Phase (40%)

At approximately **40%** she spawns a group of spiders. Once this happens pretty much ignore the sacs and focus the burn on the mob. A **zerker** or strong AE'ing **offtank** needs to try to get as many spiders beating on them as possible.

> **NOTE:** The spider adds are immune to all taunts. Another option if you have multiple **Enchanters** is to mezz them. Either way you want to burn Tairiza as fast as possible once the adds start. If you have big spells/CAs with long recast timers, make sure you save them for that final push.

## Final Burn

Once the spiders pop everyone in the raid target the named directly and just **BURN BURN BURN**. Some people will die. They need to revive and get back in. AE's will burn a lot of the spiders down, but prolly not all of them so once you kill the named kill the remaining spiders and loot the **metal chest**.
